#!/bin/bash

# =============================================================================
# loop.sh - Ralph Wiggum Loop Orchestrator
# =============================================================================
#
# Usage:
#   ./loop.sh                    # Run with defaults
#   ./loop.sh --max-iterations 30  # Custom iteration limit
#   ./loop.sh --dry-run           # Show what would run without executing
#
# =============================================================================

set -e

# Configuration
MAX_ITERATIONS=${MAX_ITERATIONS:-50}
SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS=${SLEEP_BETWEEN:-2}
LOG_FILE="ralph-session-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log"

# Parse arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
        --max-iterations)
            MAX_ITERATIONS="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        --dry-run)
            DRY_RUN=1
            shift
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown option: $1"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m'

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Logging
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

log() {
    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    echo -e "[$timestamp] $1" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_phase() {
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    log "$1"
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pre-flight Checks
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

preflight_checks() {
    log_phase "Pre-flight Checks"
    
    # Check required files exist
    local required_files=("PROMPT.md" "AGENTS.md" "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" "scripts/validate-migration.sh")
    for file in "${required_files[@]}"; do
        if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
            log "${RED}ERROR: Required file missing: $file${NC}"
            exit 1
        fi
    done
    log "${GREEN}✓ All required files present${NC}"
    
    # Check validation script is executable
    chmod +x scripts/validate-migration.sh
    log "${GREEN}✓ Validation script executable${NC}"
    
    # Check claude-code is available
    if ! command -v claude-code &> /dev/null; then
        if ! command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
            log "${RED}ERROR: claude-code not found in PATH${NC}"
            exit 1
        fi
        CLAUDE_CMD="claude"
    else
        CLAUDE_CMD="claude-code"
    fi
    log "${GREEN}✓ Claude command available: $CLAUDE_CMD${NC}"
    
    # Check we're in a git repo
    if [ ! -d ".git" ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}WARNING: Not a git repository. Changes won't be tracked.${NC}"
    else
        log "${GREEN}✓ Git repository detected${NC}"
    fi
    
    # Check source/target directories
    if [ ! -d "$HOME/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place" ]; then
        log "${RED}ERROR: Source directory not found${NC}"
        exit 1
    fi
    log "${GREEN}✓ Source directory exists${NC}"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main Loop
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

run_iteration() {
    local iteration=$1
    
    log_phase "Iteration $iteration / $MAX_ITERATIONS"
    
    # Run Claude with the prompt
    log "Invoking Claude..."
    
    local output
    if [ -n "$DRY_RUN" ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}DRY RUN: Would execute: $CLAUDE_CMD -p --dangerously-skip-permissions < PROMPT.md${NC}"
        output="DRY_RUN_OUTPUT"
    else
        output=$($CLAUDE_CMD -p --dangerously-skip-permissions < PROMPT.md 2>&1) || true
    fi
    
    # Log output (truncated)
    echo "$output" | tail -100 >> "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Check for completion signals
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>"; then
        log "${GREEN}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        log "${GREEN}  ALL TASKS COMPLETE!${NC}"
        log "${GREEN}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        return 0
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>STUCK</promise>"; then
        log "${YELLOW}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        log "${YELLOW}  AGENT IS STUCK - Manual intervention required${NC}"
        log "${YELLOW}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        echo "$output" | grep -A 10 "<promise>STUCK</promise>" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
        return 2
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>UNSAFE_REQUIRED</promise>"; then
        log "${RED}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        log "${RED}  UNSAFE CHANGE REQUIRED - Human decision needed${NC}"
        log "${RED}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        echo "$output" | grep -A 10 "<promise>UNSAFE_REQUIRED</promise>" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
        return 3
    fi
    
    # Run validation as additional backpressure
    # Write to file so next iteration can read what failed
    log "Running validation..."
    local VALIDATION_OUTPUT="validation_results.txt"
    ./scripts/validate-migration.sh > "$VALIDATION_OUTPUT" 2>&1
    local validation_exit=$?
    cat "$VALIDATION_OUTPUT" >> "$LOG_FILE"

    if [ $validation_exit -ne 0 ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}Validation failed - results saved to $VALIDATION_OUTPUT for next iteration${NC}"
    else
        log "${GREEN}Validation passed${NC}"
        # Clear validation results file on success
        echo "VALIDATION PASSED at $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')" > "$VALIDATION_OUTPUT"
    fi

    # Continue to next iteration
    return 1
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

main() {
    log_phase "Ralph Wiggum Loop Starting"
    log "Max iterations: $MAX_ITERATIONS"
    log "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
    
    preflight_checks
    
    local iteration=0
    local exit_code=1
    
    while [ $iteration -lt $MAX_ITERATIONS ] && [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; do
        iteration=$((iteration + 1))
        
        run_iteration $iteration
        exit_code=$?
        
        if [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; then
            log "Sleeping ${SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS}s before next iteration..."
            sleep $SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS
        fi
    done
    
    log_phase "Loop Finished"
    
    if [ $iteration -ge $MAX_ITERATIONS ] && [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}Reached maximum iterations ($MAX_ITERATIONS)${NC}"
        log "Review progress and run again if needed."
        exit 1
    fi
    
    case $exit_code in
        0)
            log "${GREEN}Migration completed successfully!${NC}"
            exit 0
            ;;
        2)
            log "${YELLOW}Agent stuck after $iteration iterations.${NC}"
            exit 2
            ;;
        3)
            log "${RED}Unsafe change required. Human decision needed.${NC}"
            exit 3
            ;;
    esac
}

main "$@"

# Implementation Plan: Source-Faithful Migration

**Source**: `~/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/`
**Target**: `~/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/`
**Last Updated**: 2026-01-23

---

## Current Status: 🔴 BEHAVIORAL CHANGES DETECTED

Previous migration introduced behavioral changes. This plan reverts them while preserving style guide compliance where safe.

---

## Phase 0: Verify Unmapped Files

These files exist in target but NOT in source. Decision required.

- [ ] **TASK-000a**: Verify `repository/airlineMiles.repository.ts`
  - Status: EXISTS in target, NOT in source
  - Decision needed: Delete (not in source) OR Document as intentional new feature
  - Files: `repository/airlineMiles.repository.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-000b**: Verify `hooks/useInvoiceCashbackPermissionStore.ts`
  - Status: EXISTS in target, NOT in source
  - Decision needed: Delete OR Document as intentional addition
  - Files: `hooks/useInvoiceCashbackPermissionStore.ts`

---

## Phase 1: Critical Reversions (UNSAFE → SAFE)

These tasks revert behavioral changes back to source behavior.

### High Priority — State Shape Changes

- [ ] **TASK-001**: Revert `invoicesCashbackPermission.model.ts` state shape
  - Source: `{ hasRedeemed: boolean, status: PermissionStatus }`
  - Target: `{ hasRedeemed: boolean, isLoading: boolean, isError: boolean }`
  - Action: Restore `PermissionStatus` enum and `status` field
  - Files: `models/invoicesCashbackPermission.model.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-002a**: Revert `invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts` to source pattern
  - Source: Uses `setStatus()` action with enum
  - Target: Uses `setLoading()`, `setError()` separate actions
  - Action: Restore source action pattern
  - Files: `store/invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-002b**: Fix filename typo (`invoives` → `invoices`)
  - Depends on: TASK-002a (revert content first, then rename)
  - Action: Rename file and update all imports
  - Files: `store/invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts` → `store/invoicesCashbackPermission.store.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-003**: Revert `parameter.model.ts` — remove new logic
  - Source: No `pointsQuantity` field, no `extractPointsQuantityFromParameterization()`
  - Target: Has both
  - Action: Remove added field and function (or move to separate file if needed elsewhere)
  - Files: `models/parameter.model.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-004**: Revert `parameter.store.ts` — remove computed property
  - Source: `setData()` just saves data
  - Target: `setData()` also computes `pointsQuantity`
  - Action: Remove computation, match source exactly
  - Files: `store/parameter.store.ts`

### High Priority — Service Behavior Changes

- [ ] **TASK-005**: Revert `invoicesCashbackPermission.service.ts` to update store
  - Source: Service calls store actions (`setStatus`, `setHasRedeemed`)
  - Target: Service only returns data, doesn't update store
  - Action: Restore store update calls in service
  - Files: `services/invoicesCashbackPermission.service.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-006**: Revert `useInvoiceCashbackPermission.ts` hook
  - Source: Derives `isLoading`/`isError` from `status` enum
  - Target: Reads `isLoading`/`isError` directly from store
  - Also: Remove `showLoading` parameter (doesn't exist in source)
  - Files: `hooks/useInvoiceCashbackPermission.ts`

### High Priority — API/Repository Pattern Changes

- [ ] **TASK-007**: Revert `invoicesCashbackPermission.api.ts` pattern
  - Source: Function exports returning `Promise<AxiosResponse<T>>`
  - Target: Class instance with unwrapped responses
  - Action: Restore function pattern with raw AxiosResponse
  - Files: `api/invoicesCashbackPermission.api.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-008**: Revert `invoicesCashbackPermission.repository.ts` pattern
  - Source: Simple function `mapPermissionResponse()`
  - Target: Class with multiple methods
  - Action: Restore function pattern
  - Files: `repository/invoicesCashbackPermission.repository.ts`

### Medium Priority — Model Changes

- [ ] **TASK-009**: Revert `extract.model.ts` changes
  - Source: `TransactionType` has 3 values, `PointsTransaction` has `status` field
  - Target: `TransactionType` has 4 values (added REFUNDED), `status` field missing
  - Action: Restore source definition exactly
  - Files: `models/extract.model.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-010**: Revert `extract.repository.ts` — restore status mapping
  - Source: `getTransactionFromDto()` includes `status: dto.status`
  - Target: `status` field missing from mapping
  - Action: Restore status field mapping
  - Files: `repository/extract.repository.ts`

---

## Phase 2: Safe Style Alignment

These tasks apply style guide changes that DON'T affect behavior.

### Import Path Standardization

- [ ] **TASK-011**: Verify all `~/store` imports
  - Ensure no relative store imports remain
  - Files: All files in `services/`

- [ ] **TASK-012**: Update `zustand/shallow` imports
  - Change `zustand/shallow` to `zustand/react/shallow`
  - Files: All hooks using `useShallow`

### Filename Corrections

- [ ] **TASK-013**: Fix `invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts` typo
  - Rename to `invoicesCashbackPermission.store.ts`
  - Update all imports referencing this file
  - Note: Do this AFTER TASK-002 (revert content first)

### Index File Updates

- [ ] **TASK-014**: Update `store/index.ts` exports
  - Ensure exports match source structure
  - Update after filename corrections

- [ ] **TASK-015**: Update `models/index.ts` exports
  - Ensure all model exports present
  - Match source export pattern

---

## Phase 3: Verification

- [ ] **TASK-016**: Full diff audit
  - Run `validate-migration.sh` on all file pairs
  - Document any remaining differences
  - Classify as SAFE or requiring human decision

- [ ] **TASK-017**: Final typecheck
  - Run `npm run typecheck`
  - Fix any type errors introduced by reversions

---

## Discovered Work

<!-- Add items discovered during implementation -->

---

## Completed Tasks

<!-- Move completed tasks here with date -->

---

## Blocked Tasks

<!-- Document any blockers -->

---

## Human Decisions Required

<!-- Questions that need human input -->

- [ ] Is `pointsQuantity` used elsewhere in the app? If so, where should this logic live?
- [ ] The REFUNDED transaction type in target — is this a new feature or should it be removed?
- [ ] Confirm: Should we match source exactly, even if target pattern seems "better"?

---

## Notes

### Source of Truth
The source repository (`card-place`) is the source of truth. Any deviation from source behavior is a bug unless explicitly approved.

### Testing Strategy
Since no automated tests exist:
1. Rely on TypeScript for structural correctness
2. Rely on `validate-migration.sh` for behavioral equivalence
3. Any remaining verification must be manual

### Risk Areas
1. Permission system has the most changes — highest risk
2. Extract model changes could affect transaction display
3. Parameter store changes could affect points display

Now I understand the context:

1. **loop.sh** - An orchestrator that runs Claude Code iteratively with a PROMPT.md file to perform migration tasks. It logs to a session log file and checks for completion signals.

2. **IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md** - Lists 17 tasks across 3 phases to revert behavioral changes in the migration from `rnm-app-fintech-card-place` to this project.

3. **progress.txt** - Session log showing the initial setup was done, but Phase 1 hasn't started.

4. **features.json** - JSON tracking of all tasks, all currently "pending".

The user wants me to:
1. Modify loop.sh to log Claude Code iterations in real-time
2. Execute it and fix everything until migration is complete

Let me read additional files to understand the full context - PROMPT.md, validation script, and the source files.

content.content:
1→# Migration Task Instructions
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→You are migrating code from `rnm-app-fintech-card-place` (source) to `rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram` (target).
     6→
     7→**CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS:**
     8→- Source code is PRODUCTION and QA-TESTED
     9→- No new manual QA will be performed
    10→- No reliable automated tests exist
    11→- You MUST preserve source behavior exactly
    12→
    13→Read these files before starting:
    14→- `AGENTS.md` — Build commands, SAFE/UNSAFE rules, gotchas
    15→- `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` — Current task list and status
    16→- `specs/migration-rules.md` — Detailed rules for what's allowed
    17→- `specs/file-mapping.md` — Source to target file mapping
    18→
    19→---
    20→
    21→## Your Task
    22→
    23→### Step 1: Read Current State
    24→
    25→1. **Check validation results from previous iteration** (if exists):
    26→   - Read `validation_results.txt` to understand what failed last time
    27→   - Address those specific issues first
    28→
    29→2. **Read task status**:
    30→   - Read `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` to see current progress
    31→   - Read `AGENTS.md` for rules and gotchas
    32→
    33→### Step 2: Select ONE Task
    34→- Pick the highest-priority incomplete task from the plan
    35→- Respect dependencies (don't start if blockers aren't done)
    36→- ONE task per iteration — do not combine tasks
    37→
    38→### Step 3: Before Any Code Changes
    39→
    40→For the selected file pair (source → target):
    41→
    42→1. **Read source file completely**
    43→2. **Read target file completely**
    44→3. **Identify ALL differences** (not just the obvious ones)
    45→4. **Classify each difference as SAFE or UNSAFE**
    46→
    47→If UNSAFE differences exist:
    48→- Revert target to match source behavior
    49→- Apply only SAFE transformations (import paths, file organization)
    50→
    51→### Step 4: Implement
    52→
    53→- Make the minimal changes necessary
    54→- Preserve source behavior exactly
    55→- Apply style guide only where it doesn't change behavior
    56→- No "improvements" or "bug fixes"
    57→
    58→### Step 5: Validate
    59→
    60→#### 5.1 Structural Validation
    61→```bash
    62→./scripts/validate-migration.sh
    63→```
    64→This checks file existence and generates `comparison_manifest.json`.
    65→
    66→#### 5.2 Semantic Validation (3 Agents + 1 Judge)
    67→
    68→For each file pair in the task, use the Task tool to spawn **3 parallel comparison agents**:
    69→
    70→**Agent 1 - Interface Analyst:**
    71→```
    72→Compare interfaces and types between source and target.
    73→Source: [source_file_path]
    74→Target: [target_file_path]
    75→
    76→Report:
    77→- List all interfaces/types in source
    78→- List all interfaces/types in target
    79→- Flag ANY field differences (added, removed, type changed)
    80→- Verdict: IDENTICAL / HAS_CHANGES
    81→```
    82→
    83→**Agent 2 - Function Analyst:**
    84→```
    85→Compare exported functions between source and target.
    86→Source: [source_file_path]
    87→Target: [target_file_path]
    88→
    89→Report:
    90→- List all exported functions with signatures
    91→- Flag ANY signature differences (params, return types)
    92→- Flag ANY logic differences in function bodies
    93→- Verdict: IDENTICAL / HAS_CHANGES
    94→```
    95→
    96→**Agent 3 - State Analyst:**
    97→```
    98→Compare Zustand store state shape and actions.
    99→Source: [source_file_path]
   100→Target: [target_file_path]
   101→
   102→Report:
   103→- List state shape (all fields and types)
   104→- List all actions/methods
   105→- Flag ANY differences
   106→- Verdict: IDENTICAL / HAS_CHANGES
   107→```
   108→
   109→**Then spawn Judge Agent:**
   110→```
   111→You are the judge. Review the 3 analyst reports.
   112→
   113→Reports:
   114→[paste agent 1 result]
   115→[paste agent 2 result]
   116→[paste agent 3 result]
   117→
   118→Determine:
   119→1. Are ALL changes SAFE (import paths, file organization only)?
   120→2. Or are there UNSAFE changes (behavior, state shape, signatures)?
   121→
   122→Final Verdict: SAFE / UNSAFE
   123→If UNSAFE, list specific changes that must be reverted.
   124→```
   125→
   126→#### 5.3 TypeScript Check
   127→```bash
   128→npm run typecheck
   129→```
   130→
   131→**Do NOT proceed to Step 6 unless ALL validations pass.**
   132→
   133→### Step 6: Update State
   134→
   135→After validation passes:
   136→
   137→1. Mark task complete in `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`
   138→2. Add discovered work if any
   139→3. Commit changes:
   140→```bash
   141→git add -A
   142→git commit -m "migrate: [filename] - preserve source behavior"
   143→```
   144→
   145→---
   146→
   147→## Rules
   148→
   149→### What You CAN Do
   150→- Change import paths for new directory structure
   151→- Use `~/store` alias (mandatory)
   152→- Fix filename typos
   153→- Split `index.tsx` into `index.ts` + `view.tsx` (if behavior preserved)
   154→- Update `zustand/shallow` to `zustand/react/shallow`
   155→
   156→### What You CANNOT Do
   157→- Add/remove function parameters
   158→- Change return types
   159→- Add/remove interface fields
   160→- Change state shapes
   161→- Add computed properties
   162→- Change enum values
   163→- "Fix" anything that would change runtime behavior
   164→
   165→### When In Doubt
   166→If unsure whether a change is SAFE:
   167→- It's UNSAFE
   168→- Keep source behavior
   169→- Document the question in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
   170→
   171→---
   172→
   173→## Exit Conditions
   174→
   175→### Task Complete, More Remain
   176→Exit normally. The loop will restart you with fresh context.
   177→
   178→### All Tasks Complete
   179→```
   180→<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>
   181→```
   182→
   183→### Stuck (After 3 Failed Attempts)
   184→```
   185→<promise>STUCK</promise>
   186→
   187→Reason: [describe the issue]
   188→File: [which file]
   189→Attempts: [what you tried]
   190→```
   191→
   192→### Unsafe Change Required
   193→If completing a task REQUIRES an unsafe change:

# Migration Task Instructions

## Context

You are migrating code from `rnm-app-fintech-card-place` (source) to `rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram` (target).

**CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS:**
- Source code is PRODUCTION and QA-TESTED
- No new manual QA will be performed
- No reliable automated tests exist
- You MUST preserve source behavior exactly

Read these files before starting:
- `AGENTS.md` — Build commands, SAFE/UNSAFE rules, gotchas
- `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` — Current task list and status
- `specs/migration-rules.md` — Detailed rules for what's allowed
- `specs/file-mapping.md` — Source to target file mapping

---

## Your Task

### Step 1: Read Current State

1. **Check validation results from previous iteration** (if exists):
   - Read `validation_results.txt` to understand what failed last time
   - Address those specific issues first

2. **Read task status**:
   - Read `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` to see current progress
   - Read `AGENTS.md` for rules and gotchas

### Step 2: Select ONE Task
- Pick the highest-priority incomplete task from the plan
- Respect dependencies (don't start if blockers aren't done)
- ONE task per iteration — do not combine tasks

### Step 3: Before Any Code Changes

For the selected file pair (source → target):

1. **Read source file completely**
2. **Read target file completely**
3. **Identify ALL differences** (not just the obvious ones)
4. **Classify each difference as SAFE or UNSAFE**

If UNSAFE differences exist:
- Revert target to match source behavior
- Apply only SAFE transformations (import paths, file organization)

### Step 4: Implement

- Make the minimal changes necessary
- Preserve source behavior exactly
- Apply style guide only where it doesn't change behavior
- No "improvements" or "bug fixes"

### Step 5: Validate

#### 5.1 Structural Validation
```bash
./scripts/validate-migration.sh
```
This checks file existence and generates `comparison_manifest.json`.

#### 5.2 Semantic Validation (3 Agents + 1 Judge)

For each file pair in the task, use the Task tool to spawn **3 parallel comparison agents**:

**Agent 1 - Interface Analyst:**
```
Compare interfaces and types between source and target.
Source: [source_file_path]
Target: [target_file_path]

Report:
- List all interfaces/types in source
- List all interfaces/types in target
- Flag ANY field differences (added, removed, type changed)
- Verdict: IDENTICAL / HAS_CHANGES
```

**Agent 2 - Function Analyst:**
```
Compare exported functions between source and target.
Source: [source_file_path]
Target: [target_file_path]

Report:
- List all exported functions with signatures
- Flag ANY signature differences (params, return types)
- Flag ANY logic differences in function bodies
- Verdict: IDENTICAL / HAS_CHANGES
```

**Agent 3 - State Analyst:**
```
Compare Zustand store state shape and actions.
Source: [source_file_path]
Target: [target_file_path]

Report:
- List state shape (all fields and types)
- List all actions/methods
- Flag ANY differences
- Verdict: IDENTICAL / HAS_CHANGES
```

**Then spawn Judge Agent:**
```
You are the judge. Review the 3 analyst reports.

Reports:
[paste agent 1 result]
[paste agent 2 result]
[paste agent 3 result]

Determine:
1. Are ALL changes SAFE (import paths, file organization only)?
2. Or are there UNSAFE changes (behavior, state shape, signatures)?

Final Verdict: SAFE / UNSAFE
If UNSAFE, list specific changes that must be reverted.
```

#### 5.3 TypeScript Check
```bash
npm run typecheck
```

**Do NOT proceed to Step 6 unless ALL validations pass.**

### Step 6: Update State

After validation passes:

1. Mark task complete in `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`
2. Add discovered work if any
3. Commit changes:
```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "migrate: [filename] - preserve source behavior"
```

---

## Rules

### What You CAN Do
- Change import paths for new directory structure
- Use `~/store` alias (mandatory)
- Fix filename typos
- Split `index.tsx` into `index.ts` + `view.tsx` (if behavior preserved)
- Update `zustand/shallow` to `zustand/react/shallow`

### What You CANNOT Do
- Add/remove function parameters
- Change return types
- Add/remove interface fields
- Change state shapes
- Add computed properties
- Change enum values
- "Fix" anything that would change runtime behavior

### When In Doubt
If unsure whether a change is SAFE:
- It's UNSAFE
- Keep source behavior
- Document the question in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md

---

## Exit Conditions

### Task Complete, More Remain
Exit normally. The loop will restart you with fresh context.

### All Tasks Complete
```
<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>
```

### Stuck (After 3 Failed Attempts)
```
<promise>STUCK</promise>

Reason: [describe the issue]
File: [which file]
Attempts: [what you tried]
```

### Unsafe Change Required
If completing a task REQUIRES an unsafe change:
```
<promise>UNSAFE_REQUIRED</promise>

File: [which file]
Required Change: [what needs to change]
Why Source Behavior Cannot Be Preserved: [explanation]
```

---

## Validation Script Failures

### "BEHAVIORAL CHANGE DETECTED"
The script found a difference in function signatures, interfaces, or state shapes.
- Read the diff output
- Identify which change caused it
- Revert that change
- Keep only SAFE changes

### "MISSING SOURCE FILE"
The file exists in target but not in source.
- This might be a new feature (acceptable)
- Or a renamed file (check mapping)
- Skip validation for truly new files

### "TYPE MISMATCH"
Interface or type definition differs.
- Revert to source definition exactly
- Import path changes are OK
- Field changes are NOT OK

---

## Remember

1. **Source is truth** — When in doubt, match source exactly
2. **No heroics** — Don't fix bugs, don't improve code
3. **One task at a time** — Fresh context prevents drift
4. **Validate before commit** — Script failures mean STOP and FIX
5. **Document uncertainty** — Better to flag than to guess wrong

#!/bin/bash

# =============================================================================
# validate-migration.sh
# Backpressure script for Ralph loop - detects behavioral changes
# =============================================================================

set -e

SOURCE_DIR="${SOURCE_DIR:-$HOME/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram}"
TARGET_DIR="${TARGET_DIR:-$HOME/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services}"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

ERRORS_FOUND=0
WARNINGS_FOUND=0

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Utility Functions
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

log_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}❌ ERROR: $1${NC}"
    ERRORS_FOUND=$((ERRORS_FOUND + 1))
}

log_warning() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠️  WARNING: $1${NC}"
    WARNINGS_FOUND=$((WARNINGS_FOUND + 1))
}

log_success() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}✅ $1${NC}"
}

log_info() {
    echo -e "   $1"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# File Comparison Strategy
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
#
# Pattern matching for TypeScript is unreliable. Instead:
# 1. This script does basic structural checks (file existence, unmapped files)
# 2. Actual semantic comparison is done by Claude agents in PROMPT.md
# 3. Uses "3 agents + 1 judge" pattern for reliable comparison
#
# The bash script flags files that NEED comparison, Claude does the comparison.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

generate_comparison_manifest() {
    local manifest_file="comparison_manifest.json"

    echo "{" > "$manifest_file"
    echo '  "generated_at": "'$(date -Iseconds)'",' >> "$manifest_file"
    echo '  "source_dir": "'$SOURCE_DIR'",' >> "$manifest_file"
    echo '  "target_dir": "'$TARGET_DIR'",' >> "$manifest_file"
    echo '  "file_pairs": [' >> "$manifest_file"

    local first=true
    local i=0
    for source_rel in "${SOURCE_FILES[@]}"; do
        local target_rel="${TARGET_FILES[$i]}"
        local source_file="$SOURCE_DIR/$source_rel"
        local target_file="$TARGET_DIR/$target_rel"

        if [ -f "$source_file" ] && [ -f "$target_file" ]; then
            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                first=false
            else
                echo "," >> "$manifest_file"
            fi
            echo -n '    {"source": "'$source_rel'", "target": "'$target_rel'"}' >> "$manifest_file"
        fi
        i=$((i + 1))
    done

    echo "" >> "$manifest_file"
    echo "  ]," >> "$manifest_file"
    echo '  "unmapped_files": [' >> "$manifest_file"

    # Add unmapped files
    first=true
    while IFS= read -r target_file; do
        local rel_path="${target_file#$TARGET_DIR/}"
        if ! is_mapped_target "$rel_path" && ! is_approved_new_file "$rel_path"; then
            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                first=false
            else
                echo "," >> "$manifest_file"
            fi
            echo -n '    "'$rel_path'"' >> "$manifest_file"
        fi
    done < <(find "$TARGET_DIR" -name "*.ts" -o -name "*.tsx" 2>/dev/null)

    echo "" >> "$manifest_file"
    echo "  ]" >> "$manifest_file"
    echo "}" >> "$manifest_file"

    log_success "Generated comparison manifest: $manifest_file"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Approved New Files (not in source, intentionally added)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

APPROVED_NEW_FILES=(
    # Marketplace feature (new)
    "api/marketplace.api.ts"
    "models/marketplace.model.ts"
    "repository/marketplace.repository.ts"
    "services/marketplace.service.ts"
    "store/marketplace.store.ts"
    "hooks/useMarketplace.ts"
    # Products feature (new)
    "api/products.api.ts"
    "models/products.model.ts"
    "repository/products.repository.ts"
    "services/products.service.ts"
    "store/products.store.ts"
    "hooks/useProducts.ts"
    # Rescue feature (new)
    "api/rescue.api.ts"
    "models/rescue.model.ts"
    "services/rescue.service.ts"
    "store/rescue.store.ts"
    # Utilities (new)
    "utils/index.ts"
    "utils/participantId.ts"
    "moment.ts"
    "constants.ts"
    # Index files
    "api/index.ts"
    "models/index.ts"
    "repository/index.ts"
    "services/index.ts"
    "store/index.ts"
    "hooks/index.ts"
)

is_approved_new_file() {
    local rel_path="$1"
    for approved in "${APPROVED_NEW_FILES[@]}"; do
        if [ "$rel_path" = "$approved" ]; then
            return 0
        fi
    done
    return 1
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# File Mapping (Source → Target)
# Using parallel arrays for bash 3.x compatibility (macOS)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

SOURCE_FILES=(
    # Models
    "models/parameter.model.ts"
    "models/extract.model.ts"
    "models/airlineMiles.model.ts"
    "models/permission.model.ts"
    # API
    "api/parameter.api.ts"
    "api/extract.api.ts"
    "api/airlineMiles.api.ts"
    "api/permission.api.ts"
    # Repository
    "repository/parameter.repository.ts"
    "repository/extract.repository.ts"
    "repository/permission.repository.ts"
    # Services
    "services/parameter.service.ts"
    "services/extract.service.ts"
    "services/airlineMiles.service.ts"
    "services/permission.service.ts"
    # Store
    "store/parameter.store.ts"
    "store/extract.store.ts"
    "store/airlineMiles.store.ts"
    "store/permission.store.ts"
    # Hooks
    "hooks/useAirlineMiles.ts"
    "hooks/usePermission.ts"
)

TARGET_FILES=(
    # Models
    "models/parameter.model.ts"
    "models/extract.model.ts"
    "models/airlineMiles.model.ts"
    "models/invoicesCashbackPermission.model.ts"
    # API
    "api/parameter.api.ts"
    "api/extract.api.ts"
    "api/airlineMiles.api.ts"
    "api/invoicesCashbackPermission.api.ts"
    # Repository
    "repository/parameter.repository.ts"
    "repository/extract.repository.ts"
    "repository/invoicesCashbackPermission.repository.ts"
    # Services
    "services/parameter.service.ts"
    "services/extract.service.ts"
    "services/airlineMiles.service.ts"
    "services/invoicesCashbackPermission.service.ts"
    # Store
    "store/parameter.store.ts"
    "store/extract.store.ts"
    "store/airlineMiles.store.ts"
    "store/invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts"
    # Hooks
    "hooks/useAirlineMiles.ts"
    "hooks/useInvoiceCashbackPermission.ts"
)

get_target_for_source() {
    local source="$1"
    local i=0
    for src in "${SOURCE_FILES[@]}"; do
        if [ "$src" = "$source" ]; then
            echo "${TARGET_FILES[$i]}"
            return 0
        fi
        i=$((i + 1))
    done
    return 1
}

is_mapped_target() {
    local target="$1"
    for tgt in "${TARGET_FILES[@]}"; do
        if [ "$tgt" = "$target" ]; then
            return 0
        fi
    done
    return 1
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Validation Functions (Structural Only)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
#
# Semantic comparison (interfaces, enums, functions) is done by Claude agents.
# This script only does structural validation:
# - File existence
# - Unmapped files detection
# - Generate manifest for agent comparison
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

validate_file_pair() {
    local source_rel="$1"
    local target_rel="$2"

    local source_file="$SOURCE_DIR/$source_rel"
    local target_file="$TARGET_DIR/$target_rel"

    # Check if source exists
    if [ ! -f "$source_file" ]; then
        log_warning "Source file not found: $source_rel"
        return 0
    fi

    # Check if target exists
    if [ ! -f "$target_file" ]; then
        log_error "Target file missing: $target_rel (expected from $source_rel)"
        return 1
    fi

    # Basic check: files exist and are readable
    log_success "File pair exists: $source_rel → $target_rel"
    return 0
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Check for Unmapped Files in Target (potential UNSAFE additions)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

check_unmapped_target_files() {
    echo ""
    echo "Checking for unmapped files in target..."
    echo ""

    local unmapped_count=0

    # Find all TypeScript files in target
    while IFS= read -r target_file; do
        local rel_path="${target_file#$TARGET_DIR/}"

        # Check if this file is a mapped target or an approved new file
        if is_mapped_target "$rel_path"; then
            : # File is mapped, OK
        elif is_approved_new_file "$rel_path"; then
            log_info "New file (approved): $rel_path"
        else
            log_error "UNMAPPED FILE in target: $rel_path (no source equivalent, not approved)"
            unmapped_count=$((unmapped_count + 1))
        fi
    done < <(find "$TARGET_DIR" -name "*.ts" -o -name "*.tsx" 2>/dev/null)

    if [ $unmapped_count -gt 0 ]; then
        echo ""
        echo "Found $unmapped_count unmapped file(s). These may be UNSAFE additions."
        echo "Either add them to APPROVED_NEW_FILES or delete them."
    fi
}


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main Validation
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

echo "=============================================="
echo "  Migration Validation Script"
echo "=============================================="
echo ""
echo "Source: $SOURCE_DIR"
echo "Target: $TARGET_DIR"
echo ""

# Check directories exist
if [ ! -d "$SOURCE_DIR" ]; then
    log_error "Source directory not found: $SOURCE_DIR"
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -d "$TARGET_DIR" ]; then
    log_error "Target directory not found: $TARGET_DIR"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Phase 1: Structural validation (file existence)..."
echo ""

# Validate each mapped file pair exists
i=0
for source_rel in "${SOURCE_FILES[@]}"; do
    target_rel="${TARGET_FILES[$i]}"
    validate_file_pair "$source_rel" "$target_rel"
    i=$((i + 1))
done

echo ""
echo "Phase 2: Checking for unmapped files..."

# Check for unmapped files in target (potential UNSAFE additions)
check_unmapped_target_files

echo ""
echo "Phase 3: Generating comparison manifest for agent validation..."

# Generate manifest for Claude agent comparison
generate_comparison_manifest

echo ""
echo "=============================================="
echo "  Structural Validation Summary"
echo "=============================================="

if [ $ERRORS_FOUND -gt 0 ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}STRUCTURAL ERRORS: $ERRORS_FOUND error(s) found${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo "Fix structural issues first:"
    echo "1. Missing target files must be created"
    echo "2. Unmapped files must be approved or removed"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

if [ $WARNINGS_FOUND -gt 0 ]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}STRUCTURAL OK with $WARNINGS_FOUND warning(s)${NC}"
else
    echo -e "${GREEN}STRUCTURAL OK: All files exist${NC}"
fi

echo ""
echo "=============================================="
echo "  Next Step: Semantic Validation"
echo "=============================================="
echo ""
echo "Manifest generated: comparison_manifest.json"
echo ""
echo "Claude should now run semantic comparison using:"
echo "  - 3 parallel comparison agents (interfaces, functions, state)"
echo "  - 1 judge agent to evaluate SAFE vs UNSAFE"
echo ""
echo "See PROMPT.md for agent comparison instructions."
echo ""

exit 0

# File Mapping: Source → Target

## Overview

This document maps files from the source repository to their target locations.

**Target**: `~/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/`

## Naming Convention Changes

| `usePermission` | `useInvoiceCashbackPermission` | Follows above |
| `PermissionState` | `InvoiceCashbackPermissionState` | Follows above |

**Important**: The rename is cosmetic. Behavior MUST match source.

content.content:
1→# Migration Rules Specification
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→This document defines what changes are permitted during the migration from `rnm-app-fintech-card-place` to `rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram`.
     6→
     7→**Core Principle**: Source behavior must be preserved exactly. The source code is production and QA-tested. No new testing will be performed.
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## SAFE Changes (Allowed)
    12→
    13→These changes do NOT affect runtime behavior and ARE permitted.
    14→
    15→### 1. Import Path Adjustments
    16→
    17→```typescript
    18→// Source (card-place structure)
    19→import { something } from '../../../../utils';
    20→import { store } from '../../store';
    21→
    22→// Target (rewardprogram structure) - SAFE
    23→import { something } from '../../utils';
    24→import { store } from '~/store';  // Alias required by style guide
    25→```
    26→
    27→**Rule**: Path changes due to different directory structure are SAFE.
    28→
    29→### 2. Store Alias Usage
    30→
    31→```typescript
    32→// Source (relative path)
    33→import { useRootStore } from '../../store';
    34→
    35→// Target (alias) - SAFE and REQUIRED
    36→import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
    37→```
    38→
    39→**Rule**: The `~/store` alias is mandatory per style guide. This is SAFE.
    40→
    41→### 3. Zustand Import Path Update
    42→
    43→```typescript
    44→// Source (old path)
    45→import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
    46→
    47→// Target (new path) - SAFE
    48→import { useShallow } from 'zustand/react/shallow';
    49→```
    50→
    51→**Rule**: Zustand module reorganization. Same functionality, different path.
    52→
    53→### 4. Filename Typo Fixes
    54→
    55→```
    56→// Source
    57→invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts  ← typo
    58→
    59→// Target - SAFE
    60→invoicesCashbackPermission.store.ts  ← corrected
    61→```
    62→
    63→**Rule**: Fixing obvious typos in filenames is SAFE. Update all imports accordingly.
    64→
    65→### 5. File Organization (Hook Component Pattern)
    66→
    67→```
    68→// Source (combined file)
    69→screens/Feature/index.tsx  ← contains both logic and render
    70→
    71→// Target (split) - SAFE if behavior identical
    72→screens/Feature/
    73→├── index.ts    ← logic hook only
    74→├── view.tsx    ← render only
    75→└── Models/
    76→    └── index.d.ts
    77→```
    78→
    79→**Rule**: Splitting files is SAFE if:
    80→- All logic moves to hook
    81→- All render moves to view
    82→- Types move to Models
    83→- NO behavioral changes in the process
    84→
    85→### 6. Removing JSDoc Comments
    86→
    87→```typescript
    88→// Source
    89→/**
    90→ * This function does something.
    91→ * @param {string} id - The ID
    92→ * @returns {Promise<Data>} The data
    93→ */
    94→export const getData = (id: string): Promise<Data> => { ... }
    95→
    96→// Target - SAFE
    97→export const getData = (id: string): Promise<Data> => { ... }
    98→```
    99→
   100→**Rule**: Removing JSDoc comments is SAFE. They don't affect runtime.
   101→
   102→### 7. Adding Console Logging
   103→
   104→```typescript
   105→// Source
   106→} catch (error) {
   107→  throw error;
   108→}
   109→
   110→// Target - SAFE (console only)
   111→} catch (error) {
   112→  console.error('getData failed:', error);
   113→  throw error;
   114→}
   115→```
   116→
   117→**Rule**: Adding `console.log/warn/error` is SAFE. It doesn't change behavior.
   118→
   119→### 8. Whitespace and Formatting
   120→
   121→```typescript
   122→// Any formatting changes that don't affect logic are SAFE
   123→```
   124→
   125→**Rule**: Prettier/ESLint formatting changes are SAFE.
   126→
   127→---
   128→
   129→## UNSAFE Changes (Forbidden)
   130→
   131→These changes AFFECT runtime behavior and are NOT permitted.
   132→
   133→### 1. Function Signature Changes
   134→
   135→```typescript
   136→// Source
   137→export const checkHasRedeemed = (params: { forceRefresh?: boolean }) => { ... }
   138→
   139→// UNSAFE - New parameter added
   140→export const checkHasRedeemed = (params: { showLoading?: boolean; forceRefresh?: boolean }) => { ... }
   141→```
   142→
   143→**Rule**: NEVER add, remove, or modify function parameters.
   144→
   145→### 2. Return Type Changes
   146→
   147→```typescript
   148→// Source
   149→getPermissionApi(): Promise<AxiosResponse<PermissionDocumentResponse>>
   150→
   151→// UNSAFE - Different return type
   152→getPermission(): Promise<{ data: PermissionDocument }>
   153→```
   154→
   155→**Rule**: Return types must match source exactly.
   156→
   157→### 3. Interface Field Changes
   158→
   159→```typescript
   160→// Source
   161→interface PermissionState {
   162→  hasRedeemed: boolean;
   163→  status: PermissionStatus;
   164→}
   165→
   166→// UNSAFE - Different fields
   167→interface InvoiceCashbackPermissionState {
   168→  hasRedeemed: boolean;
   169→  isLoading: boolean;  // NOT in source
   170→  isError: boolean;    // NOT in source
   171→}
   172→```
   173→
   174→**Rule**: Interface fields must match source exactly.
   175→
   176→### 4. State Shape Changes
   177→
   178→```typescript
   179→// Source (uses enum)
   180→rewardsPermission: {
   181→  hasRedeemed: boolean,
   182→  status: PermissionStatus.IDLE | LOADING | SUCCESS | ERROR
   183→}
   184→
   185→// UNSAFE - Different shape
   186→invoiceCashbackPermission: {
   187→  hasRedeemed: boolean,
   188→  isLoading: boolean,
   189→  isError: boolean
   190→}
   191→```
   192→
   193→**Rule**: State shapes must match source exactly.
   194→
   195→### 5. Enum Value Changes
   196→
   197→```typescript
   198→// Source
   199→type TransactionType = 'CREDIT' | 'RESCUE' | 'EXPIRED';
   200→
   201→// UNSAFE - New value added
   202→type TransactionType = 'CREDIT' | 'RESCUE' | 'EXPIRED' | 'REFUNDED';
   203→```
   204→
   205→**Rule**: Enum/union values must match source exactly.
   206→
   207→### 6. New Computed Properties
   208→
   209→```typescript
   210→// Source (setData just saves)
   211→setData: (data) => set({ parameterPointsProgram: { ...state, data } })
   212→
   213→// UNSAFE - Adds computation
   214→setData: (data) => set({ 
   215→  parameterPointsProgram: { 
   216→    ...state, 
   217→    data,
   218→    pointsQuantity: extractPointsQuantity(data)  // NOT in source
   219→  } 
   220→})
   221→```
   222→
   223→**Rule**: Do not add computed properties that don't exist in source.
   224→
   225→### 7. Responsibility Shifts
   226→
   227→```typescript
   228→// Source - Service updates store
   229→export const checkHasRedeemed = async () => {
   230→  setStatus(PermissionStatus.LOADING);
   231→  const result = await api.get();
   232→  setHasRedeemed(result);
   233→  setStatus(PermissionStatus.SUCCESS);
   234→}
   235→
   236→// UNSAFE - Service doesn't update store (moved to hook)
   237→export const hasRedeemed = async () => {
   238→  const result = await api.get();
   239→  return result;  // No store update
   240→}
   241→```
   242→
   243→**Rule**: Keep responsibilities where source has them.
   244→
   245→### 8. Logic Changes
   246→
   247→```typescript
   248→// Source
   249→const isRescueConcluded = !isRescueFailed && !!rescueReducer?.status;
   250→
   251→// UNSAFE - Different logic
   252→const isRescueConcluded = rescueStatus === RescueStatus.SUCCESS && !!rescueResponse;
   253→```
   254→
   255→**Rule**: Logic must be identical to source.
   256→
   257→### 9. Class vs Function Pattern Changes
   258→
   259→```typescript
   260→// Source - Function exports
   261→export const getPermissionApi = () => api.get('/permission');
   262→export const savePermissionApi = (data) => api.post('/permission', data);
   263→
   264→// UNSAFE - Class pattern
   265→class PermissionApi {
   266→  getPermission() { return api.get('/permission'); }
   267→  savePermission(data) { return api.post('/permission', data); }
   268→}
   269→export const permissionApi = new PermissionApi();
   270→```
   271→
   272→**Rule**: Keep the same pattern as source.
   273→
   274→---
   275→
   276→## Decision Matrix
   277→
   278→| Change Type | SAFE? | Reason |
   279→|-------------|-------|--------|
   280→| Import paths | ✅ | Different directory structure |
   281→| `~/store` alias | ✅ | Style guide requirement |
   282→| zustand import path | ✅ | Library reorganization |
   283→| Filename typos | ✅ | Obvious corrections |
   284→| Hook Component split | ✅ | If behavior preserved |
   285→| Remove JSDoc | ✅ | No runtime effect |
   286→| Add console.log | ✅ | No behavior change |
   287→| Add parameter | ❌ | Changes function signature |
   288→| Remove parameter | ❌ | Changes function signature |
   289→| Change return type | ❌ | Changes contract |
   290→| Add interface field | ❌ | Changes state shape |
   291→| Remove interface field | ❌ | Changes state shape |
   292→| Add enum value | ❌ | Changes type domain |
   293→| Change logic | ❌ | Changes behavior |
   294→| Add computed property | ❌ | New behavior |
   295→| Shift responsibility | ❌ | Changes when things happen |
   296→
   297→---
   298→
   299→## When In Doubt
   300→
   301→If you're unsure whether a change is SAFE:
   302→
   303→1. **Assume it's UNSAFE**
   304→2. **Keep source behavior exactly**
   305→3. **Document the question** in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md under "Human Decisions Required"
   306→4. **Do not proceed** with the uncertain change
   307→
   308→Better to be too conservative than to break production.
   309→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

input.new_string:
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main Loop
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

run_iteration() {
    local iteration=$1

    log_phase "Iteration $iteration / $MAX_ITERATIONS"

    # Run Claude with the prompt - REAL-TIME LOGGING
    log "Invoking Claude (streaming output in real-time)..."

    local output
    local exit_status=0
    local ITERATION_LOG="iteration-${iteration}-$(date +%H%M%S).log"

    if [ -n "$DRY_RUN" ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}DRY RUN: Would execute: $CLAUDE_CMD -p --dangerously-skip-permissions < PROMPT.md${NC}"
        output="DRY_RUN_OUTPUT"
    else
        # Real-time logging: use tee to show output AND save to file simultaneously
        # Using script command for proper unbuffered output on macOS
        if command -v script &> /dev/null && [[ "$OSTYPE" == "darwin"* ]]; then
            # macOS: use script for unbuffered output
            script -q "$ITERATION_LOG" $CLAUDE_CMD -p --dangerously-skip-permissions < PROMPT.md 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" || exit_status=$?
        else
            # Linux/other: use stdbuf for unbuffered output
            stdbuf -oL -eL $CLAUDE_CMD -p --dangerously-skip-permissions < PROMPT.md 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" "$ITERATION_LOG" || exit_status=$?
        fi

        # Read the full output for signal detection
        output=$(cat "$ITERATION_LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        # Keep iteration log for debugging
        log "Iteration log saved to: $ITERATION_LOG"
    fi

    # Log output summary
    log "Output captured ($(echo "$output" | wc -l | tr -d ' ') lines)"

#!/bin/bash

# =============================================================================
# loop.sh - Ralph Wiggum Loop Orchestrator
# =============================================================================
#
# Usage:
#   ./loop.sh                    # Run with defaults
#   ./loop.sh --max-iterations 30  # Custom iteration limit
#   ./loop.sh --dry-run           # Show what would run without executing
#
# =============================================================================

set -e

# Configuration
MAX_ITERATIONS=${MAX_ITERATIONS:-50}
SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS=${SLEEP_BETWEEN:-2}
LOG_FILE="ralph-session-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log"

# Parse arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
        --max-iterations)
            MAX_ITERATIONS="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        --dry-run)
            DRY_RUN=1
            shift
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown option: $1"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m'

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Logging
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

log() {
    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    echo -e "[$timestamp] $1" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_phase() {
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    log "$1"
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pre-flight Checks
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

preflight_checks() {
    log_phase "Pre-flight Checks"
    
    # Check required files exist
    local required_files=("PROMPT.md" "AGENTS.md" "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" "scripts/validate-migration.sh")
    for file in "${required_files[@]}"; do
        if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
            log "${RED}ERROR: Required file missing: $file${NC}"
            exit 1
        fi
    done
    log "${GREEN}✓ All required files present${NC}"
    
    # Check validation script is executable
    chmod +x scripts/validate-migration.sh
    log "${GREEN}✓ Validation script executable${NC}"
    
    # Check claude-code is available
    if ! command -v claude-code &> /dev/null; then
        if ! command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
            log "${RED}ERROR: claude-code not found in PATH${NC}"
            exit 1
        fi
        CLAUDE_CMD="claude"
    else
        CLAUDE_CMD="claude-code"
    fi
    log "${GREEN}✓ Claude command available: $CLAUDE_CMD${NC}"
    
    # Check we're in a git repo
    if [ ! -d ".git" ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}WARNING: Not a git repository. Changes won't be tracked.${NC}"
    else
        log "${GREEN}✓ Git repository detected${NC}"
    fi
    
    # Check source/target directories
    if [ ! -d "$HOME/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place" ]; then
        log "${RED}ERROR: Source directory not found${NC}"
        exit 1
    fi
    log "${GREEN}✓ Source directory exists${NC}"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main Loop
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

run_iteration() {
    local iteration=$1
    
    log_phase "Iteration $iteration / $MAX_ITERATIONS"
    
    # Run Claude with the prompt
    log "Invoking Claude..."
    
    local output
    if [ -n "$DRY_RUN" ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}DRY RUN: Would execute: $CLAUDE_CMD -p --dangerously-skip-permissions < PROMPT.md${NC}"
        output="DRY_RUN_OUTPUT"
    else
        output=$($CLAUDE_CMD -p --dangerously-skip-permissions < PROMPT.md 2>&1) || true
    fi
    
    # Log output (truncated)
    echo "$output" | tail -100 >> "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Check for completion signals
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>"; then
        log "${GREEN}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        log "${GREEN}  ALL TASKS COMPLETE!${NC}"
        log "${GREEN}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        return 0
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>STUCK</promise>"; then
        log "${YELLOW}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        log "${YELLOW}  AGENT IS STUCK - Manual intervention required${NC}"
        log "${YELLOW}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        echo "$output" | grep -A 10 "<promise>STUCK</promise>" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
        return 2
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>UNSAFE_REQUIRED</promise>"; then
        log "${RED}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        log "${RED}  UNSAFE CHANGE REQUIRED - Human decision needed${NC}"
        log "${RED}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        echo "$output" | grep -A 10 "<promise>UNSAFE_REQUIRED</promise>" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
        return 3
    fi
    
    # Run validation as additional backpressure
    # Write to file so next iteration can read what failed
    log "Running validation..."
    local VALIDATION_OUTPUT="validation_results.txt"
    ./scripts/validate-migration.sh > "$VALIDATION_OUTPUT" 2>&1
    local validation_exit=$?
    cat "$VALIDATION_OUTPUT" >> "$LOG_FILE"

    if [ $validation_exit -ne 0 ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}Validation failed - results saved to $VALIDATION_OUTPUT for next iteration${NC}"
    else
        log "${GREEN}Validation passed${NC}"
        # Clear validation results file on success
        echo "VALIDATION PASSED at $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')" > "$VALIDATION_OUTPUT"
    fi

    # Continue to next iteration
    return 1
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

main() {
    log_phase "Ralph Wiggum Loop Starting"
    log "Max iterations: $MAX_ITERATIONS"
    log "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
    
    preflight_checks
    
    local iteration=0
    local exit_code=1
    
    while [ $iteration -lt $MAX_ITERATIONS ] && [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; do
        iteration=$((iteration + 1))
        
        run_iteration $iteration
        exit_code=$?
        
        if [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; then
            log "Sleeping ${SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS}s before next iteration..."
            sleep $SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS
        fi
    done
    
    log_phase "Loop Finished"
    
    if [ $iteration -ge $MAX_ITERATIONS ] && [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}Reached maximum iterations ($MAX_ITERATIONS)${NC}"
        log "Review progress and run again if needed."
        exit 1
    fi
    
    case $exit_code in
        0)
            log "${GREEN}Migration completed successfully!${NC}"
            exit 0
            ;;
        2)
            log "${YELLOW}Agent stuck after $iteration iterations.${NC}"
            exit 2
            ;;
        3)
            log "${RED}Unsafe change required. Human decision needed.${NC}"
            exit 3
            ;;
    esac
}

main "$@"

1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    12→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    13→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    15→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    16→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    17→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    18→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    19→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    20→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    21→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    22→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Introduction
    27→
    28→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    29→
    30→Key decisions:
    31→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    32→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    33→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    34→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    39→
    40→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    41→
    42→```
    43→screens/Home/
    44→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    45→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    46→└── Models/
    47→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    48→```
    49→
    50→**Models/index.d.ts**
    51→```ts
    52→export interface HomeParams {
    53→  welcome?: string;
    54→}
    55→export interface HomeState {
    56→  greeting: string;
    57→  isLoading: boolean;
    58→}
    59→export interface HomeHandlers {
    60→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    61→}
    62→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    63→```
    64→
    65→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    66→```ts
    67→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from "react";
    68→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from "./Models";
    69→import { useUserStore } from "@/services/user/store"; // Zustand slice example
    70→
    71→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    72→  const name = useUserStore((s) => s.name);
    73→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    74→
    75→  useEffect(() => {
    76→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    77→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    78→  }, []);
    79→
    80→  const greeting = useMemo(
    81→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || "guest"}!`,
    82→    [params?.welcome, name]
    83→  );
    84→
    85→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    86→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    87→  }, []);
    88→
    89→  return { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA };
    90→}
    91→```
    92→
    93→**view.tsx (render)**
    94→```tsx
    95→import React from "react";
    96→import { View } from "react-native";
    97→import { Text, Button } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
    98→import { useHomeScreen } from "./index";
    99→import type { HomeParams } from "./Models";
   100→
   101→type Props = HomeParams;
   102→
   103→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   104→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   105→
   106→  if (isLoading) {
   107→    return (
   108→      <View>
   109→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   110→      </View>
   111→    );
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  return (
   115→    <View>
   116→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   117→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   118→    </View>
   119→  );
   120→}
   121→```
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   126→
   127→```
   128→atomic/atoms/Button/
   129→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   130→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   131→└── Models/
   132→    └── index.d.ts
   133→```
   134→
   135→**Models/index.d.ts**
   136→```ts
   137→export interface ButtonProps {
   138→  text: string;
   139→  disabled?: boolean;
   140→  onPress?: () => void;
   141→}
   142→export interface ButtonState {
   143→  pressed: boolean;
   144→}
   145→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   146→  handlePress: () => void;
   147→}
   148→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   149→```
   150→
   151→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   152→```ts
   153→import { useCallback, useState } from "react";
   154→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from "./Models";
   155→
   156→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   157→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   158→
   159→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   160→    setPressed(true);
   161→    props.onPress?.();
   162→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   163→  }, [props.onPress]);
   164→
   165→  return { pressed, handlePress };
   166→}
   167→```
   168→
   169→**view.tsx (render)**
   170→```tsx
   171→import React from "react";
   172→import { Pressable, View } from "react-native";
   173→import { Text } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
   174→import { useButton } from "./index";
   175→import type { ButtonProps } from "./Models";
   176→
   177→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   178→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   179→
   180→  return (
   181→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   182→      <View>
   183→        <Text>{props.text}{pressed ? " •" : ""}</Text>
   184→      </View>
   185→    </Pressable>
   186→  );
   187→}
   188→```
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## Project Organization
   193→
   194→```
   195→src/
   196→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   197→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   198→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   199→│   ├── atoms/
   200→│   ├── molecules/
   201→│   └── organisms/
   202→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   203→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   204→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   205→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   206→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   207→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   208→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   209→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   210→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   211→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   212→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   213→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   214→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   215→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   216→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   217→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   218→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   219→```
   220→
   221→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Services Architecture
   226→
   227→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   228→
   229→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   230→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   231→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   232→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   233→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   234→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   235→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   236→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   237→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   238→
   239→Principles:
   240→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   241→- No circular dependencies.
   242→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   247→
   248→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   249→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   250→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   251→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   252→
   253→**Slice example**
   254→```ts
   255→import type { StateCreator } from "zustand";
   256→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from "../models/store";
   257→
   258→export type AuthSlice = {
   259→    auth: AuthState;
   260→    authActions: AuthActions
   261→};
   262→
   263→const initialAuth = {
   264→    user: null,
   265→    loading: false,
   266→    error: undefined,
   267→}
   268→
   269→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<AuthSlice, [], [], AuthSlice> = (set) => ({
   270→    auth: initialAuth,
   271→    authActions: {
   272→        setLoading: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, isLoading: v } })),
   273→        setUser: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, user: v } })),
   274→        setError: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, error: v } })),
   275→        reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   276→    }
   277→});
   278→```
   279→
   280→**Root Store**
   281→
   282→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   283→
   284→```ts
   285→import { create } from "zustand";
   286→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";
   287→import { immer } from "zustand/middleware/immer";
   288→
   289→import { createUserSlice, type UserSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user";
   290→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-yourmf";
   291→
   292→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   293→
   294→/** Add your slice Type */
   295→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   296→
   297→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   298→  devtools(
   299→    persist(
   300→      immer((...a) => ({
   301→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   302→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   303→      })),
   304→      {
   305→        name: "root-storage",
   306→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   307→        partialize: (s) => ({
   308→          user: s.user,
   309→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   310→        }),
   311→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({ ...current, ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>) }),
   312→      }
   313→    ),
   314→    { name: "RootStore" }
   315→  )
   316→);
   317→```
   318→
   319→- For Use on Class.
   320→
   321→```ts
   322→class Auth {
   323→  private readonly setAuth;
   324→
   325→  constructor () {
   326→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   327→  }
   328→
   329→  function doSomething(data) {
   330→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   331→  }
   332→}
   333→```
   334→
   335→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   336→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   337→
   338→```ts
   339→const loading = useRootStore((s) => s.user.isLoading);
   340→const token = useRootStore((s) => s.auth.token);
   341→```
   342→
   343→---
   344→
   345→## Routes Naming
   346→
   347→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   348→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   349→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   350→
   351→---
   352→
   353→## Folder Naming Rules
   354→
   355→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                    |
   356→|-------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------|
   357→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails`  |
   358→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`    |
   359→
   360→---
   361→
   362→## Code Standards
   363→
   364→**Language**: English only.  
   365→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   366→
   367→**Explanatory variables**
   368→```js
   369→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   370→const getNumberValidation = (message) => (value) => !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   371→const isNumber = getNumberValidation("error message");
   372→isNumber(1);
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Semantic constants**
   376→```js
   377→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   378→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   379→```
   380→
   381→**Library workarounds with link**
   382→```js
   383→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   384→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   385→event.target.value = "";
   386→```
   387→
   388→**JSDoc**
   389→```js
   390→/**
   391→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   392→ * @param {*} eventName
   393→ * @param {*} maxLength
   394→ */
   395→```
   396→
   397→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   398→```ts
   399→useEffect(() => {
   400→  (async () => {
   401→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   402→    setSum(42);
   403→  })();
   404→}, [
   405→  setSum,
   406→]);
   407→```
   408→
   409→**Extract conditions**
   410→```ts
   411→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   412→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   413→```
   414→
   415→**Optional chaining**
   416→```ts
   417→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   418→```
   419→
   420→**JSX indentation**
   421→```tsx
   422→{
   423→  !isEnd && (
   424→    <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />
   425→  )
   426→}
   427→```
   428→
   429→**Early returns**
   430→```ts
   431→if (!data) return null;
   432→return <View />;
   433→```
   434→
   435→**Nullish coalescing**
   436→```ts
   437→return data ?? 0;
   438→```
   439→---
   440→
   441→## Styles Patterns
   442→
   443→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   444→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→## Cache Pattern
   449→
   450→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   451→
   452→```ts
   453→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   454→
   455→export interface CachedData<T> {
   456→  timestamp: number;
   457→  data: T;
   458→}
   459→
   460→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   461→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   462→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   463→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   464→}
   465→
   466→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   467→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   468→
   469→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   470→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   471→    if (!json) return null;
   472→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   473→  }
   474→
   475→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   476→    const payload: CachedData<T> = { timestamp: Date.now(), data };
   477→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   478→  }
   479→
   480→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   481→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   482→  }
   483→}
   484→```
   485→
   486→**Guidelines**
   487→- One storage class per module/feature.
   488→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   489→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   490→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   491→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   492→
   493→---
   494→
   495→## Checklist
   496→
   497→- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   498→- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   499→- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   500→- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   501→- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   502→- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   503→- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   504→
   505→---
   506→

# AGENTS.md

## Project Overview
React Native microfrontend migration. Source is **PRODUCTION/QA-TESTED**. No new QA, no automated tests.

## Directories
```bash
SOURCE=~/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram
TARGET=~/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services
```

## Critical Patterns

**Store imports (MANDATORY):**
```typescript
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';  // ✅ Always use alias
// import { useRootStore } from '../../store';  // ❌ Never relative
```

**Zustand:** `zustand/shallow` → `zustand/react/shallow`

## SAFE vs UNSAFE

| SAFE (Allowed) | UNSAFE (Forbidden) |
|----------------|-------------------|
| Import path changes | Function signature changes |
| `~/store` alias | Interface field changes |
| Filename typo fixes | State shape changes |
| zustand path update | Enum value changes |
| File organization | New computed properties |
| Remove JSDoc | Any logic changes |

## Known Gotchas

1. **Permission → InvoiceCashbackPermission**: Rename OK, but behavior must match source. If target uses `isLoading/isError` instead of `PermissionStatus` enum, that's UNSAFE.

2. **pointsQuantity**: `extractPointsQuantityFromParameterization()` doesn't exist in source. Remove or isolate.

3. **Service vs Hook**: Source services update store directly. If target moved this to hooks, that's UNSAFE.

## File Naming
- Components: `PascalCase.tsx`
- Hooks: `use*.ts`
- Services: `*.service.ts`
- Stores: `*.store.ts`
- Models: `*.model.ts`

# The Ralph Book

## The Definitive Guide to Autonomous AI Coding Loops

> Geoffrey Huntley and the Ralph Wiggum Technique

**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** January 23, 2026

---

## Preface

This document represents the most comprehensive reference on the Ralph Wiggum technique for autonomous AI-assisted software development. It synthesizes primary sources from Geoffrey Huntley, community documentation, academic research, and real-world production experience.

**Reading Paths:**

- **Quick Start:** Important Notices → How It Works → Best Practices
- **Deep Understanding:** Origin Story → Philosophy → Context Engineering → RPI Loop
- **Production Deployment:** Security → Principal Skinner → Enterprise Compliance → Recovery
- **Tool Selection:** State-of-the-Art Alternatives → Decision Tree
- **Implementation Mastery:** File Templates → PRD Format → Tasks.json → Implementation Plan Creation

---

## Table of Contents

### Part I: Foundation

1. [**Important Notices (January 2026)**](#important-notices-january-2026) ⚠️
2. [Origin Story](#origin-story)
3. [**The Two Ralphs: Huntley vs. Anthropic**](#the-two-ralphs-huntley-vs-anthropic) ⚠️
4. [Philosophy & Theoretical Basis](#philosophy--theoretical-basis)

### Part II: Core Methodology

5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [The Five-File Architecture](#the-five-file-architecture)
7. [The Three-Phase Workflow](#the-three-phase-workflow)
8. [**The RPI Loop (Advanced)**](#the-rpi-loop-advanced)
9. [Context Engineering](#context-engineering)
10. [Backpressure: The Key to Convergence](#backpressure-the-key-to-convergence)

### Part III: Production Considerations

11. [**Security Considerations**](#security-considerations) ⚠️
12. [**The Principal Skinner Pattern**](#the-principal-skinner-pattern)
13. [**Claude Code 2.1.x: New Capabilities**](#claude-code-21x-new-capabilities) ✨ _UPDATED_
14. [**Enterprise Compliance**](#enterprise-compliance)
15. [**Production Risks & Unknown Unknowns**](#production-risks--unknown-unknowns)

### Part IV: Practical Implementation

16. [Official Claude Code Plugin](#official-claude-code-plugin)
17. [**Cost Modeling**](#cost-modeling)
18. [Best Practices & Tips](#best-practices--tips)
19. [Common Mistakes & Anti-Patterns](#common-mistakes--anti-patterns)
20. [**Troubleshooting Guide**](#troubleshooting-guide)
21. [**Recovery Procedures**](#recovery-procedures)

### Part V: File Specifications & Templates ✨ _NEW_

22. [**Complete File Architecture**](#complete-file-architecture)
23. [**PRD Format Specification**](#prd-format-specification)
24. [**tasks.json Format Specification**](#tasksjson-format-specification)
25. [**IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Creation Guide**](#implementation_planmd-creation-guide)
26. [**AGENTS.md Template**](#agentsmd-template)
27. [**PROMPT Templates**](#prompt-templates)
28. [**specs/ Directory Structure**](#specs-directory-structure)

### Part VI: Tool Integration ✨ _NEW_

29. [**TaskMaster AI Integration**](#taskmaster-ai-integration)
30. [**MCP Server Configuration**](#mcp-server-configuration)
31. [**Multi-Tool Workflows**](#multi-tool-workflows)

### Part VII: Ecosystem

32. [Real-World Results](#real-world-results)

34. [**Tool Selection Decision Tree**](#tool-selection-decision-tree)
